text: As a child, you were praised whenever you obeyed strict rules.
As a teenager, you watched a friend suffer after being publicly shamed.
In early adulthood, you succeeded by following authority without question.
You remember regretting a moment when you ignored your conscience.
A family memory taught you that calm responsibility protects everyone.
You once lost trust in someone who used power carelessly.
A mentor told you that procedures matter most under emotional stress.
You remember being helpless when no one intervened in time.
You have a proud memory of refusing to harm someone despite pressure.
A painful breakup taught you to avoid causing emotional damage.
